task:
ATOM 0 — Pin the current ticketing-surface damage with a FAILING test (red-before-green). This atom adds proof only; it must NOT repair any ticket and must NOT change any runtime/production source. Later atoms turn it green.

WHAT TO BUILD:
Add one new test file (suggested: packages/core/tests/tickets-surface-consistency.test.ts) that reads the REAL live repo tree (cocoder/tickets/** and cocoder/priorities/**), not a fixture, and asserts two invariants over the actual tickets:

  INV-1 (no surface divergence — objective condition 1): The set of OPEN ticket ids agrees across all three derived surfaces:
    (a) open ticket files: cocoder/tickets/open/NNNN-*.md (4-digit id prefix),
    (b) cocoder/tickets/order.json (a JSON array of id strings),
    (c) the rows under the '## Open' section of cocoder/tickets/INDEX.md.
    The three id sets must be EQUAL. Fail with a message naming the ids that appear in one surface but not another.

  INV-2 (no stale archived-priority binding — objective condition 4 end-state): No OPEN ticket's `priority:` frontmatter value resolves to an ARCHIVED priority. A priority slug is 'active' iff cocoder/priorities/<slug>.md exists; it is 'archived' iff it exists only under cocoder/priorities/archive/<slug>.md. A `priority:` of none/unassigned/empty is fine (standalone). Fail naming each open ticket id whose priority points at an archived priority.

USE the existing core ticket loader where natural (packages/core/src/tickets/loader.ts exposes readTickets / loadTicket; priority.ts exposes normalizeTicketPriority) rather than re-parsing frontmatter by hand — but keep this strictly read-only.

EXPECTED RESULT (this is the point — it MUST be RED now):
  - INV-1 fails: order.json = ["0085","0082","0083","0084","0086"] and files include 0082-0086, but INDEX.md '## Open' currently lists only 0086 and 0082 — so 0083, 0084, 0085 are missing from INDEX.
  - INV-2 fails: tickets 0082, 0083, 0084, 0085 all carry `priority: local-cache-retention`, which is archived (cocoder/priorities/archive/local-cache-retention.md; no active cocoder/priorities/local-cache-retention.md).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
  - The new test FAILS when run, and its failure messages explicitly name: missing-from-INDEX ids (0083/0084/0085) for INV-1, and the archived-bound ids (0082/0083/0084/0085) for INV-2. A green result is a FAIL of this atom — it must be red for these documented reasons.
  - No production/runtime source file is modified. No ticket file, INDEX.md, order.json, or any governance file is modified. Diff is the single new test file only.
  - Every OTHER existing test in the suite still passes (the new test must not perturb existing greens).

WRITE SCOPE: packages/core/tests/** only (the one new test file). Do not touch packages/core/src/**, cocoder/**, or any other path.

EXIT CRITERION (one-shot, not loop-amenable): Run the core package test command (e.g. `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test`, or the repo's standard test invocation for that package — name the exact command you ran). Report: the exact command, the new test's FAILING output showing both invariants red with the named ids, and confirmation the rest of the suite is unaffected. Paste the relevant failing assertion output as evidence.

Do NOT attempt to fix the divergence or repoint any ticket in this atom — that is downstream work behind the hardened governed paths.

ATOM 1 — Establish the binding-vs-provenance ticket SCHEMA and the single rule that owns it. This atom is ADDITIVE and must NOT rewire any create caller (triage.ts, CLI, daemon routes, authoring-queue) — that caller migration is the next atom. Keeping atom 1 additive means existing create paths keep working unchanged.

MODEL (already founder-approved via this priority's Objective condition 3 + ticket 0086): A ticket's BINDING to a priority is deliberate and carries a one-line reason; standalone (no binding) is the DEFAULT. PROVENANCE (the creating run/priority) is recorded SEPARATELY and is purely informational — it never implies a binding.

WHAT TO BUILD (core only, packages/core/src/tickets/**):
1. Schema fields. Extend the ticket frontmatter contract so a ticket can carry, in addition to the existing `priority:` (which now means ONLY the deliberate binding target; `none`/empty = standalone):
   - `binding-reason:` — a one-line reason, present iff `priority:` is a real (non-standalone) binding.
   - `provenance:` — a single informational line recording the creating run/priority (e.g. `run_279 (ticketing-paths-hardening)`). Never parsed as a binding.
   Author these in `compose.ts`: add OPTIONAL `bindingReason?: string | null` and `provenance?: string | null` to `ComposeTicketMarkdownInput`, and emit a `binding-reason:` line only when a non-empty reason is supplied and a `provenance:` line only when supplied. Do NOT change the existing required `priority` field or break the current call signature for callers that don't pass the new fields.
2. Loader. Extend the `Ticket` type in `loader.ts` with `bindingReason: string | null` and `provenance: string | null`, parsed (scalar) from frontmatter `binding-reason:` and `provenance:`. Legacy tickets lacking these fields parse them as null. Keep `priority` parsing as-is.
3. ONE owner of the rule. Add `packages/core/src/tickets/binding.ts` as the SINGLE source of truth for 'a binding is deliberate and carries a reason'. Export a small pure helper, e.g. `validateBinding({ priority, reason })` returning a typed result/throwing a typed error, encoding: standalone (priority none/empty) ⇒ no reason needed and reason must be empty; a non-standalone binding ⇒ a non-empty reason is REQUIRED. Reuse `normalizeTicketPriority` from `priority.ts` for the standalone test. Do NOT duplicate this rule anywhere else — later atoms (create-caller migration, repoint hardening) must IMPORT this helper, not re-encode the rule.
   This helper is defined and unit-tested in this atom but NOT yet wired into createTicket/repoint — wiring is the next atom. Defining-but-not-wiring is intentional to keep this atom additive.

PROOF (extend the existing live test):
4. Add INV-3 to packages/core/tests/tickets-surface-consistency.test.ts: every OPEN ticket whose binding is non-standalone (priority not none/empty) MUST carry a non-empty `binding-reason`. Use the loader's `bindingReason`. This is RED now (0082, 0083, 0084, 0085 are bound to local-cache-retention with no binding-reason) and will go green after the cleanup atom repoints them. Keep INV-1 and INV-2 exactly as they are (still red).
5. Unit tests (packages/core/tests/**): pin the new schema round-trips and the rule:
   - compose+loader round-trip: a ticket authored with priority+bindingReason+provenance loads back with all three; a standalone ticket (priority none, no reason, no provenance) loads with bindingReason=null and provenance=null.
   - `validateBinding`: standalone with no reason → ok; standalone WITH a reason → rejected; non-standalone with non-empty reason → ok; non-standalone with empty/missing reason → rejected.

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK:
  - No create caller is modified (triage.ts, packages/cli/src/create-ticket.ts, packages/daemon/src/routes.ts, packages/daemon/src/authoring-queue.ts, oz-chat). No daemon-live refusal logic, no commit-support, no repoint/close behavior touched.
  - All existing tests stay green (the additive fields default to null/omitted). The ONLY red tests remain the live surface-consistency invariants INV-1/INV-2/INV-3.
  - Do NOT edit any cocoder/** governance file, the create-ticket Play, or the INDEX column layout. (The Play/doc update is a tracked later atom once the governed path is finalized.)

WRITE SCOPE: packages/core/src/tickets/** and packages/core/tests/** only.

EXIT CRITERION (one-shot, not loop-amenable): Run `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` and the repo typecheck (e.g. `pnpm typecheck` or `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core typecheck` — name the exact command). Report the exact commands and outputs. Evidence required: the new unit tests PASS; typecheck is clean; the live surface-consistency test is RED on exactly INV-1, INV-2, and the new INV-3 (paste the INV-3 failure naming 0082/0083/0084/0085 as having a non-standalone binding without a binding-reason); and NO other test regressed.

ATOM 2 — Flip the ticket CREATE contract to the binding/provenance model (defect 1, ticket 0086) across EVERY create caller in one atom. Core enforcement is the single choke point; because all callers ride core createTicket, they must all migrate together — a partial migration would make core throw on reasonless bindings its own callers still emit.

MODEL (from atom 1, already shipped): standalone (no binding) is the DEFAULT; a deliberate binding sets `priority:` to a real priority and MUST carry a one-line `binding-reason:`; the creating run/priority is recorded separately as `provenance:` and NEVER implies a binding. The single rule owner is `validateBinding` in packages/core/src/tickets/binding.ts — IMPORT and use it; do NOT re-encode the rule anywhere.

MANDATORY SITES (re-derived from the live tree — all are owners of the create contract; treat each as required, not optional):
1. CORE — packages/core/src/tickets/create.ts: thread `bindingReason?` and `provenance?` through CreateTicketInput into composeTicketMarkdown (compose already emits the lines). Default `priority` to standalone ('none') when not supplied. Call `validateBinding({priority, reason: bindingReason})` BEFORE writing; on a non-standalone priority without a reason it must throw (the typed TicketBindingError). The INDEX 'Priority' row cell stays the binding target (or 'none' for standalone) — unchanged column layout. This is the one enforcement choke point.
2. RUNNER — packages/core/src/runner/triage.ts (the createTicket call ~line 218): a runner-auto-filed ticket must NEVER create a reasonless binding. Record `provenance` = the CoCoder run reference (deps.runReference). Default standalone; only set a non-standalone `priority` binding when Deb's verdict deliberately recommends one, and in that case supply a `bindingReason` derived from the verdict (e.g. its summary/rationale) so validateBinding passes. Never auto-bind to the run's priority without a reason.
3. CLI — packages/cli/src/create-ticket.ts + packages/cli/src/oz-args.ts (createTicketInvocation) + the usage string in packages/cli/src/run.ts: make `--priority <slug>` the deliberate BINDING and require an accompanying `--reason <text>` whenever it is a real (non-standalone) priority; default to standalone when omitted. Auto-record `provenance` from the run context (the existing `--run <runId>` and/or its priority) as a separate field, NOT as the binding. Keep the deterministic createTicket()+commitFiles() spine — add NO new commit path.
4. DAEMON — packages/daemon/src/routes.ts (createTicketBody parser + the createTicket handler ~line 787) and packages/daemon/src/authoring-queue.ts (the createTicketCore call ~line 337): accept and pass through bindingReason + provenance; default standalone; rely on core validateBinding (surface its typed error as a clean 4xx, not a 500).
5. UI BRIDGE — packages/ui main/tickets-create.ts, renderer live.ts, and the ipc contract: update ONLY as needed so the contract change typechecks and a no-priority create stays a valid standalone create. Do not build new UI affordances here.

PIN WITH UNIT TESTS (condition 3 — 'provenance recorded separately and never auto-binds'):
  - core create.test.ts (or tickets.test.ts): default create → standalone (priority none), provenance recorded as its own frontmatter field, no binding-reason, and the run/priority is NOT written as the binding; create with a non-standalone priority but no reason → rejected with TicketBindingError; create with priority+reason → both persisted and round-trip via the loader.
  - CLI create-ticket test: `--priority` without `--reason` → rejected; `--priority`+`--reason` → binding+reason persisted; no `--priority` → standalone with provenance from `--run`.
  - daemon test (routes and/or authoring-queue): a bound create without a reason returns a clean client error (no 500/uncaught); a standalone create succeeds; provenance is stored separately.
  - Update any EXISTING create tests that assumed the old single-slot behavior to the new model — comply with it, do NOT weaken assertions.

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK / STAYS OUT OF SCOPE:
  - Do NOT touch the daemon-live create REFUSAL / governed-path-while-daemon-live routing (defect 3), commit-support (defect 2), reconcile-from-files (defect 4), repoint/close robustness (defect 5), archive reconcile (defect 6), or the create-ticket Play / founder docs. Those are later atoms.
  - Do NOT edit any cocoder/** ticket or governance file. The live surface-consistency test (INV-1/INV-2/INV-3) stays RED — this atom does not repair existing tickets.
  - Keep repoint.ts/close.ts behavior unchanged. Keep the INDEX column layout and order.json format unchanged.

WRITE SCOPE: packages/core/** (tickets + runner/triage + tests), packages/cli/** (create-ticket, oz-args, run, tests), packages/daemon/** (routes, authoring-queue, tests), packages/ui/** (only the create bridge files + their tests if the contract requires).

EXIT CRITERION (one-shot, not loop-amenable): Run the affected package suites and full typecheck — name exact commands, e.g. `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test`, `pnpm --filter @cocoder/cli test`, `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test`, `pnpm --filter @cocoder/ui test`, and `pnpm typecheck`. Report each command + result. Evidence required: all suites green EXCEPT the live surface-consistency test which stays red on exactly INV-1/INV-2/INV-3 (the existing-ticket damage, untouched here); the new create-model unit tests pass; typecheck clean across all packages. Paste the new test results and the (still-red, unchanged) INV list as evidence.

ATOM 3 — Build the core ticket-surface RECONCILE primitive and make repoint/close robust to pre-existing divergence (defects 4 and 5). CORE PACKAGE ONLY (packages/core). No governed entry points (daemon/CLI) yet — those are the next atom. Do NOT repair the live tickets and do NOT touch archive/create-path/commit-support.

BACKGROUND (verified): order.json is the OPEN-ticket ordering; INDEX.md has a derived '## Open' table and a curated '## Recently Closed' table. repointTicket() throws via setOpenTicketIndexPriority (packages/core/src/tickets/index-helpers.ts:~103 'ticket X is missing from INDEX.md Open section') exactly when a ticket is absent from INDEX — so the governed spine cannot fix the very tickets that most need fixing (defect 5). There is no governed rebuild of the derived surfaces from the files (defect 4).

BUILD:
1. ONE owner for the open INDEX row. Extract the inline open-row string built in create.ts into a single exported helper in index-helpers.ts, e.g. `openTicketIndexRow({ id, fileName, title, type, priority })` returning the exact current row format `| [id](./open/file) | title | type | priority | owner |` (escape via ticketTableCell, owner = TICKET_OWNER). Have create.ts use it. This becomes the single owner reused by reconcile and repoint-self-heal — do NOT leave a second copy of the row format anywhere.
2. NEW core reconcile primitive — packages/core/src/tickets/reconcile.ts: `reconcileTicketSurfaces({ ticketsDir, repoPath })` that rebuilds the DERIVED open surfaces from the actual open ticket files so files ↔ order.json ↔ INDEX '## Open' agree exactly:
   - Read the open ticket files (open/NNNN-*.md) and load each (readTickets/loadTicket). The binding-priority cell is the ticket's normalized priority ('none' when standalone).
   - Rebuild INDEX.md '## Open' rows: one row per open file via openTicketIndexRow; drop any '## Open' row whose id has no open file; add any open file missing a row. Leave '## Recently Closed' and the rest of INDEX.md untouched (it is curated history, not derived).
   - Rebuild order.json: keep the existing order for ids that still have an open file, append open-file ids not yet listed (ascending by id), and drop ids with no open file. Write `${JSON.stringify(ids, null, 2)}\n`.
   - Idempotent: a second run on an already-consistent tree writes identical bytes / produces no change. Return the relative paths actually written.
3. repoint robustness (defect 5) — packages/core/src/tickets/repoint.ts: when the open ticket file exists but is missing from INDEX '## Open' (and/or order.json), repointTicket must SELF-HEAL — insert the row (via openTicketIndexRow) and/or the order entry from the file and then apply the priority change — instead of throwing. After repoint the ticket must be consistent across file, INDEX row, and order.json. Reuse the shared row builder; do not duplicate the rule. Keep the existing already-at-target / missing-open-ticket results unchanged.
4. close robustness (defect 5, symmetric) — verify closeTicket tolerates a ticket missing from INDEX '## Open' (moveTicketIndexRowToClosed already no-ops a missing open row). If any divergence shape makes close throw, make it tolerant the same way. Do not change the verified-commit gate or close semantics otherwise.

PIN WITH CORE UNIT TESTS (packages/core/tests/**, use temp dirs / fixtures — NOT the live cocoder tree):
   - reconcile: a fixture whose order.json + INDEX Open diverge from the open files (id missing from INDEX, id missing from order, stale id present in INDEX/order with no file) reconciles so all three agree; a second reconcile is a no-op (idempotent); '## Recently Closed' is left byte-identical.
   - repoint divergence: a ticket present as a file but missing from INDEX Open repoints (to a bound priority WITH a reason, and to standalone) WITHOUT throwing and ends consistent across all three surfaces.
   - close divergence: closing a ticket missing from INDEX Open succeeds and leaves surfaces consistent.

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK / OUT OF SCOPE:
   - No governed entry point (daemon oz-chat, launcher, authoring-queue, CLI) is added or changed in this atom. No archive (defect 6), create-path (done in atom 2), commit-support, or daemon-live-refusal (defects 2,3) work.
   - Do NOT run reconcile against the live cocoder/tickets tree or edit any cocoder/** file. The live surface-consistency test stays RED on INV-1/INV-2/INV-3.
   - Keep INDEX column layout and order.json format unchanged. Reuse normalizeTicketPriority and validateBinding owners; add no second copy of those rules.

WRITE SCOPE: packages/core/src/tickets/** and packages/core/tests/** only.

EXIT CRITERION (one-shot, not loop-amenable): Run `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` and `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core typecheck` (name exact commands). Evidence required: the new reconcile/repoint/close-divergence unit tests PASS; typecheck clean; the live surface-consistency test still RED on exactly INV-1/INV-2/INV-3 (unchanged — this atom did not touch the live tree); no other regression. Paste the new tests' results.

ATOM 4 — Add the GOVERNED reconcile entry points AND repair a regression atom 3 introduced. FIRST, context on the regression you must fix: atom 3 made core repointTicket() enforce validateBinding (a non-standalone binding now REQUIRES a binding reason). The governed reconcile-repoint path does not thread a reason, so the branch is currently RED on 3 daemon tests (tests/reconciliation-repoint-op.test.ts 'rehomes a ticket to a live priority', tests/authoring-queue.test.ts 'closes and repoints tickets', '...keeps ticket-close errors visible'). This atom must make the whole daemon + cli suite green again AND add the reconcile-tickets governed surface.

PART A — thread the binding reason through the governed reconcile-repoint path (mirror the existing wiring; do not invent a parallel contract):
  - oz-chat (packages/daemon/src/oz-chat.ts): change the command to `reconcile-repoint <ticketId> <standalone|priorityId> [reason words...]`. For a priorityId target a reason is REQUIRED (join the remaining args); return a clear usage hint (not a 500) if absent. For standalone/none, the reason is null. Update HELP_HINT and the dispatch to pass bindingReason into ops.requestReconciliationRepoint.
  - launcher (packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts requestReconciliationRepoint, ~line 1600-1651): add `bindingReason: string | null` to its input; pass it into repointTicket(); for the inFlight branch, carry bindingReason into the enqueued 'ticket-repoint' authoring entry. Keep the existing live-priority existence guard (a bound target must be a live cocoder/priorities/<id>.md) and the commit/audit/event shape. Surface a TicketBindingError as a clean 4xx, not a 500.
  - authoring-queue (packages/daemon/src/authoring-queue.ts): add bindingReason to the queued ticket-repoint input type + validation, and pass it into repointTicket() in the ticket-repoint action.
  - Update the 3 failing daemon tests to the new model — a bound repoint now passes a reason; do NOT weaken assertions. Add coverage: a bound reconcile-repoint WITHOUT a reason returns a clean client error (no 500/uncaught); a standalone repoint still needs no reason.

PART B — add the governed reconcile-tickets surface (defect 4's invocation half), mirroring the reconcile-repoint pattern so reconcile works both daemon-LIVE and daemon-DOWN:
  - launcher: new op `requestReconciliationTickets(ctx, { workspaceId })` — if a run is inFlight for the workspace, enqueue a new authoring action 'ticket-reconcile'; otherwise call core reconcileTicketSurfaces({ ticketsDir, repoPath }) and commitFiles the returned files with message `governance: reconcile ticket surfaces (<workspaceId>)` under COCODER_GOVERNANCE_AUTHOR, append an audit + emit an event. When reconcile returns zero changed files, return ok with empty committedPaths (idempotent no-op is success, NOT an error).
  - authoring-queue: new 'ticket-reconcile' action that calls reconcileTicketSurfaces and returns its files with a `governance: reconcile queued ticket surfaces (<ws>)` message.
  - oz-chat: parse a workspace-scoped `reconcile-tickets` command (no args) → dispatch to ops.requestReconciliationTickets → a reply summarizing committed paths (or 'already consistent'). Add it to HELP_HINT.
  - CLI (packages/cli): add `cocoder oz reconcile-tickets` for the daemon-DOWN path, mirroring createTicketViaCli — a thin wrapper (e.g. packages/cli/src/reconcile-tickets.ts) that calls reconcileTicketSurfaces + commitFiles and returns the receipt; wire it in run.ts (dispatch + usage string) and oz-args.ts if an invocation parser is needed. No new commit path — reuse commitFiles.

PIN WITH TESTS:
  - daemon: a reconcile-tickets op test (a workspace fixture whose order.json/INDEX Open diverge from the open files → after the op, surfaces agree and the rebuilt files are committed; a second call is an idempotent ok no-op; an inFlight workspace enqueues 'ticket-reconcile'). Plus the Part A reason-threading coverage above.
  - cli: a reconcile-tickets test (divergent fixture → reconciled + committed via the injected git).

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK / OUT OF SCOPE:
  - Do NOT change core tickets/** logic (it is correct as of atom 3) beyond what's needed to export/consume it. Do NOT touch the create-while-daemon-live refusal or commit-support 404 (defects 2,3 — next atom), archive (defect 6), or repair the live tickets (cleanup atom). The live core surface-consistency test (INV-1/2/3) stays RED.
  - Do NOT edit any cocoder/** governance file. Keep INDEX/order formats unchanged.

WRITE SCOPE: packages/daemon/** and packages/cli/** (and packages/core/src/index.ts ONLY if a needed export is missing — reconcileTicketSurfaces is already exported).

EXIT CRITERION (one-shot, not loop-amenable): Run `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test`, `pnpm --filter @cocoder/cli test`, `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test`, and `pnpm typecheck` (name exact commands + results). Evidence required: daemon + cli suites FULLY GREEN (the 3 atom-3 reds fixed, new reconcile/reason tests passing); core unchanged (only the 3 intended live reds remain); typecheck clean across all packages. Paste the previously-failing daemon test names now passing, and the new reconcile-tickets test result.

ATOM 5 — Make `cocoder oz create-ticket` a single governed path that ALWAYS works, daemon-live or daemon-down (defect 3 / objective condition 2). Today (packages/cli/src/run.ts ~287-293) it probes the daemon and, when live, REFUSES with a dead-end ('Let the run file its own ticket, or stop the loop first') — leaving a founder-session create no governed path, which forced the off-lane Write+git and hand-authed HTTP POST during the incident. The daemon's governed create endpoint already exists and is race-safe (packages/daemon/src/routes.ts createTicket ~824: it ENQUEUES to the authoring queue when a run is inFlight, and creates+commits directly otherwise, and already supports the binding/provenance/reason body from atoms 2+4). So the fix is CLI-side routing only — mirror the EXISTING pattern that `cocoder oz close-ticket` already uses (run.ts ~207-209: probe → if live, route via a client helper to the daemon; if down, direct path).

BUILD (packages/cli only):
1. client.ts — add `createTicketViaDaemon(baseUrl, workspaceId, body)` mirroring the existing closeTicketViaDaemon/supportCommitViaDaemon helpers: POST to `/workspaces/<ws>/tickets` with a JSON body { title, type, description, ...(priority? ), ...(bindingReason?), ...(provenance?), ...(id?) }. Parse the responses the daemon actually returns: 201 { ok, ticket, committedSha } → created; 202 { ok, queued, ...receipt } → queued (a run is in flight; it will commit at the next safe boundary); 4xx → a clean error (surface the daemon's error message, e.g. a 400 TicketBindingError 'binding requires a reason'). Return a normalized result the caller can print.
2. run.ts — in the `oz create-ticket` block, REPLACE the live-daemon refusal with routing: when `live.alive`, call createTicketViaDaemon(`http://127.0.0.1:${live.port}`, 'cocoder', { title: invocation.title, type: invocation.type, description: invocation.description, ...(invocation.priority ? { priority: invocation.priority } : {}), ...(invocation.bindingReason ? { bindingReason: invocation.bindingReason } : {}), ...(runId ? { provenance: runId } : {}), ...(invocation.ticketId ? { id: invocation.ticketId } : {}) }) and print created/queued/error accordingly (queued is success, not an error). When the daemon is DOWN, keep the existing direct createTicketViaCli path unchanged. The off-lane dead-end must be gone — there is no path where a founder-session create has 'nowhere to go'.
   Use the same 'cocoder' workspaceId convention the other CLI→daemon commands use (close-ticket etc.). Keep the existing arg parsing/validation (createTicketInvocation, --priority/--reason, --run, --id) intact.

PIN WITH TESTS (packages/cli/tests, mirror the close-ticket-via-daemon tests):
   - client: createTicketViaDaemon POSTs the expected path+body and maps 201→created, 202→queued, 400→a clean error (mock fetch).
   - run/integration: with a stubbed live-daemon probe + fake responder, `oz create-ticket` ROUTES to the daemon (asserts the POST happened and the created/queued result is printed) and does NOT refuse; with the probe reporting daemon-down, it uses the direct createTicketViaCli path and commits (existing behavior preserved).

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK / OUT OF SCOPE:
   - Do NOT touch commit-support / the independent-run 404 (defect 2 — next atom), archive reconcile (defect 6), the live-ticket cleanup, or the create-ticket Play/docs. Do NOT change core or daemon logic (the daemon create endpoint already does the right thing) — CLI only, plus reusing the existing daemon endpoint.
   - Do NOT edit any cocoder/** governance file. The live core surface-consistency test stays RED on INV-1/2/3.
   - Preserve the daemon-down direct path and all existing create-ticket arg validation.

WRITE SCOPE: packages/cli/** only.

EXIT CRITERION (one-shot, not loop-amenable): Run `pnpm --filter @cocoder/cli test` and `pnpm --filter @cocoder/cli typecheck` (name exact commands + results). Also run `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test` to confirm no daemon regression (you reuse its endpoint). Evidence required: new client + routing tests pass; daemon-down path still green; daemon suite unchanged-green; typecheck clean. Paste the new test results and confirm the refusal branch is gone.

ATOM 6 — Make `cocoder oz commit-support <runId>` work for INDEPENDENT/runnerless runs (defect 2 / ticket 0085 / objective condition 2): a post-wrap support edit in an independent run must commit through a governed path with a receipt — no 404, no strand — whether the daemon is live or down.

ROOT CAUSE (confirm, then fix): `cocoder oz commit-support` (packages/cli/src/run.ts ~179-184) REQUIRES a live daemon ('no Oz daemon running — cannot commit support edits') and routes to the daemon's requestSupportCommitRun (packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts ~1355), which does `ctx.store.getRun(runId)` and returns 404 'unknown run' when the run isn't in the daemon's store. Independent runs are daemon-FREE by design (run.ts ~120 refuses run-independent while a daemon is live); a destructive independent priority (e.g. local-cache-retention, run_137) runs in an ISOLATED scratch store (resolveRunTarget / runTarget.scratchRoot), so the daemon's store never has the run — hence the 404 and the stranded support edit. First, read runStandalone / resolveRunTarget and the run store to confirm exactly where an independent run's record lives and why getRun misses it; state that in your report.

FIX (keep ONE owner of the support-commit gate; do NOT fork a second weaker path):
1. ATOM 7 — Make priority ARCHIVE reconcile its bound tickets so ZERO tickets are left pointing at the archived priority (defect 6 / objective condition 4 / acceptance 'Archiving a priority leaves zero tickets linked to it').

CURRENT BEHAVIOR (verified): the archive-priority Play moves the priority to cocoder/priorities/archive/ via a scope-gated agent turn (requestAuthoringPlay, packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts ~2322, then assertArchivePriorityMoved ~1937 verifies the move). requestArchiveConfirmation (~2274) afterward computes handledOpenTicketsForPriority and merely SURFACES them ('Handled tickets need a founder decision... NOT auto-closed'), so the bound tickets STAY linked to the now-archived priority (this is exactly why 0082-0085 still point at the archived local-cache-retention). The Play's writeScope is cocoder/priorities/** only, so the agent turn cannot touch tickets — the ticket reconcile must be a SEPARATE governed daemon commit after the move lands.

DESIGN (auto-release to standalone — non-destructive, honors 'standalone is the default' and the existing 'not auto-closed' intent; the founder keeps close/rehome as an optional follow-up):
1. CORE — add ONE owner, e.g. packages/core/src/tickets/release.ts `releaseTicketsFromArchivedPriority({ ticketsDir, repoPath, priorityId })`: for every OPEN ticket whose binding == priorityId, call the existing repointTicket({ targetPriority: null }) to release it to STANDALONE (this clears the binding + binding-reason and is divergence-robust from atom 3). PROVENANCE must be preserved — repoint must not touch the provenance line (confirm it doesn't). Return { released: readonly string[] (ids), files: readonly string[] (all changed paths, deduped) }. Reuse handledOpenTicketsForPriority/normalizeTicketPriority; do not re-encode the binding rule. Idempotent: no bound tickets → { released: [], files: [] }.
2. DAEMON — hook the release into the archive EXECUTION owner so EVERY archive path (daemon founder-confirm AND CLI `cocoder oz archive-priority`) reconciles. In requestAuthoringPlay, in the `input.playId === 'archive-priority'` branch, AFTER assertArchivePriorityMoved returns archived:true, call releaseTicketsFromArchivedPriority and, when files were changed, commitFiles them under COCODER_GOVERNANCE_AUTHOR with message `governance: release tickets from archived priority <id>` (a governed commit, separate from the Play's scoped turn), append an audit + emit an event, and attach `releasedTickets` to the returned body. If the release or its commit fails, surface it clearly (do not silently leave tickets linked).
3. Update requestArchiveConfirmation: stop surfacing handledTickets as a manual to-do; instead report the `releasedTickets` from the archive result (the tickets are already released to standalone). Update the oz-chat archiveConfirmationReply message accordingly: say N tickets were released to standalone (provenance preserved) and the founder may optionally rehome (reconcile-repoint <id> <priorityId> <reason>) or close (reconcile-close <id> <resolution>) any — NOT that they 'need a decision' before the link is cleared.

Assert provenance lines are preserved on the released tickets.
